title: VibeForge UI Screen
description: Defines a UI screen in a VibeForge application. Screen files live in app/screens/<id>.json.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference — enables editor autocomplete

id.description: Unique screen identifier matching the filename without .json (e.g. posts-list)

title.description: Page <title> shown in the browser tab

route.description: React Router path. Use :paramName for URL params (e.g. /posts/:id)

data.description: PocketBase data calls this screen runs

spec.description: json-render spec — the renderer reads this to produce the UI

root.description: ID of the root element in the elements map

elements.description: Flat map of all elements keyed by element ID

state.description: Initial state values merged into the store before rendering

filter.description: PocketBase filter expression. Use {/path} to read state at call time.

id.description: Record ID for get/update/delete. Use {"$route": "paramName"} to pull from URL.

body.description: For create/update. Values starting with / are treated as state paths; others are literals.

trigger.description: load fires on screen mount; manual fires only via a callData action

type.description: Component name from the catalog (e.g. Column, TextField)

props.description: Props passed to the component. Values can be literals or binding expressions.

children.description: Ordered list of child element IDs

actions.description: Event handlers keyed by event name (click, change, submit, rowClick, etc.)

statePath.description: State path to the array to repeat over (e.g. /data/posts/items)

key.description: Field on each item used as the React key (e.g. id)

showIf.description: Condition controlling element visibility. Use a $state expression or a boolean.

action.description: Action name registered in the catalog (callData, navigate, runBehaviour)
